title: lachnospermum umbellatum
intro: Lachnospermum umbellatum, also known as the "button daisy bush" or "Australian button daisy", is a shrub native to New South Wales and Queensland in Australia, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Its health benefits include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis, and it has also been used to soothe digestive problems. Lachnospermum umbellatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
